title: release/19.x: [InstCombine] Drop nsw in negation of select

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions nsw/nuw flags generally but all existing test examples only demonstrate nsw flag; nuw-only flag behavior is not covered in the known patterns

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i32 @negate_select_nuw_only_flag(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %c) {
  %t1 = select i1 %c, i32 0, i32 %x
  %t2 = sub i32 %y, %t1
  ret i32 %t2
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @negate_select_nuw_only_flag(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %c) {
#0:
  %t0 = sub nuw i32 0, %x
  %t1 = select i1 %c, i32 %t0, i32 %x
  %t2 = sub i32 %y, %t1
  ret i32 %t2
}
=>
define i32 @negate_select_nuw_only_flag(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %c) {
#0:
  %t1 = select i1 %c, i32 0, i32 %x
  %t2 = sub i32 %y, %t1
  ret i32 %t2
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary discusses nsw/nuw flags separately but does not cover the case where both flags are present simultaneously on the negation instruction, which requires careful handling of both poison-generating conditions

alive2.tgt:

source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i32 @negate_select_both_nsw_nuw_flags(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %c) {
  %t1 = select i1 %c, i32 0, i32 %x
  %t2 = sub i32 %y, %t1
  ret i32 %t2
}

tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i8 @negate_select_nuw_flag(i8 %x, i8 %y, i1 %c) {
  %t0 = sub i8 0, %x
  %sel = select i1 %c, i8 %y, i8 %y
  %t2 = select i1 %c, i8 %t0, i8 %x
  %result = select i1 %c, i8 %t0, i8 %x
  ret i8 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary only mentions nsw flag handling, but the patch also handles nuw flags. This test verifies nuw flag dropping works correctly.
